beliefs.gloss: Visual learning enables abstraction/inference without language.
beliefs.evidence: It is also competitive with many popular LLM-based methods.

beliefs.gloss: Visual learning enables abstraction/inference without language.
beliefs.evidence: VARC correctly solves these challenging tasks.

beliefs.gloss: Attention maps reveal pixel-to-pixel reasoning relationships.
beliefs.evidence: These attention maps show that our model can correctly reason about the relationship between a source pixel and its target pixel to copy from.

beliefs.gloss: TTT visualization shows model adapting to task-specific transformations.
beliefs.evidence: During test-time training, the prediction from Xinfer becomes progressively more accurate, with the model finally generating the correct prediction.

beliefs.gloss: Explicit 2D positional modeling preserves image structure.
beliefs.evidence: Extending from 1D positional embedding to its 2D counterpart is beneficial: see Fig. 7(b)(c).

beliefs.gloss: Explicit 2D positional modeling preserves image structure.
beliefs.evidence: Extending from 1D positional embedding to its 2D counterpart is beneficial: see Fig. 7(b)(c).

beliefs.gloss: Explicit 2D positional modeling preserves image structure.
beliefs.evidence: we replace the 2D ROPE in Fig. 7(f) with a 1D ROPE and observe a degradation of 3.5 points, from 54.5 to 51.0.

beliefs.gloss: Canvas patchification enriches data, reduces overfitting, encourages spatial priors.
beliefs.evidence: 2×2 patchification leads to 2.4 points gain, enriching data space for learning.

beliefs.gloss: Canvas patchification enriches data, reduces overfitting, encourages spatial priors.
beliefs.evidence: each patch can cover multiple colors, substantially enriching the data space for learning.

beliefs.gloss: ViT lacks inductive bias for scale invariance.
beliefs.evidence: Scale augmentation yields 6.2 points gain; ViT's lack of scale invariance explains this.

beliefs.gloss: Canvas formulation enables augmentations for translation/scale invariance.
beliefs.evidence: this setting yields an additional gain of 2.9 points (from 45.4 to 48.3).

beliefs.gloss: Canvas formulation enables augmentations for translation/scale invariance.
beliefs.evidence: Scale augmentation yields a substantial gain of 6.2 points.

beliefs.gloss: Visual common sense is learned from offline training data.
beliefs.evidence: offline training greatly improves TTT performance, suggesting visual common sense is learned.

beliefs.gloss: Some tasks are solvable from scratch (tabula rasa).
beliefs.evidence: without offline training, TTT achieves 26.4% accuracy, suggesting some tasks are solvable tabula rasa.

beliefs.gloss: Majority voting consolidates predictions from different views.
beliefs.evidence: multi-view inference further boosts to 49.8, thanks to majority voting.

beliefs.gloss: Single pixel error invalidates entire ARC prediction.
beliefs.evidence: in ARC, a mistake on even a single pixel renders the entire prediction incorrect.

beliefs.gloss: Single pixel error invalidates entire ARC prediction.
beliefs.evidence: This may explain the large gain seen here.

beliefs.gloss: Majority voting consolidates predictions from different views.
beliefs.evidence: multi-view inference further boosts to 49.8, thanks to majority voting.

beliefs.gloss: Visual learning enables abstraction/inference without language.
beliefs.evidence: VARC (18M) is ~10 points better than TRM on ARC-1, strengthening visual learning.

beliefs.gloss: Attention maps reveal pixel-to-pixel reasoning relationships.
beliefs.evidence: These attention maps show that our model can correctly reason about the relationship between a source pixel and its target pixel to copy from.

beliefs.gloss: Early layer attention reflects pattern core influence.
beliefs.evidence: early layers exhibit strong attention to 3×3 neighborhood, reflecting pattern's core influence.

beliefs.gloss: Later layer attention reflects rule for extending pixels.
beliefs.evidence: later layers (7-9) focus on outward-radiating rays, corresponding to rule extending pixels.

beliefs.gloss: Some tasks are solvable from scratch (tabula rasa).
beliefs.evidence: without offline training, TTT achieves 26.4% accuracy, suggesting some tasks are solvable tabula rasa.

beliefs.gloss: Correct predictions exist in views but are outvoted.
beliefs.evidence: model produces correct predictions in some views, but these are outvoted.

beliefs.gloss: Model handles ambiguity by proposing multiple rule interpretations.
beliefs.evidence: model attempts to interpret rule as "going-through-only" or "touching" for ambiguous tasks.

beliefs.gloss: Some ARC tasks admit multiple plausible rules.
beliefs.evidence: some may admit multiple plausible explanations or rules.

beliefs.gloss: Model handles ambiguity by proposing multiple rule interpretations.
beliefs.evidence: model attempts to interpret rule as "going-through-only" or "touching" for ambiguous tasks.
